 {"id":"bd-1vfe","title":"[EPIC] State Management & Artifacts","description":"# State Management & Artifacts

## Purpose
Enable resume capability for interrupted sweeps and capture debugging artifacts.

## Resume State File
Location: ~/.local/state/ru/agent_sweep_state.json

Schema:
{
  "run_id": "20260106-153000-12345",
  "status": "in_progress|completed|interrupted",
  "started_at": "2026-01-06T15:30:00Z",
  "config_hash": "abc123...",
  "with_release": false,
  "repos_total": 5,
  "repos_completed": ["repo1", "repo2"],
  "repos_pending": ["repo3", "repo4", "repo5"],
  "current_repo": "repo3",
  "current_phase": 2
}

## Atomic Updates
- Write to temp file, then mv (atomic)
- Always JSON-escape strings
- Prefer jq/python when available, fallback to manual

## Resume/Restart Workflow
- --resume: Load state, skip completed repos, continue
- --restart: Discard state, start fresh
- On success: Clean up state file
- On interrupt (Ctrl+C): Save state with status=interrupted

## Run Artifacts
Directory: ~/.local/state/ru/agent-sweep/runs/<run_id>/<repo>/

Artifacts per repo:
| File | Contents |
|------|----------|
| spawn.json | ntm spawn response |
| activity.ndjson | Periodic --robot-activity snapshots |
| pane_tail.txt | Last N lines from tmux pane (captured BEFORE killing) |
| commit_plan.json | Agent's commit plan output |
| release_plan.json | Agent's release plan output (if Phase 3) |
| git_before.txt | git status, log -3, branch -vv before agent |
| git_after.txt | Same after agent (for comparison) |

## Git State Capture
capture_git_state() records:
- git status
- git log -3 --oneline
- git branch -vv
- HEAD revision

## Session Preservation Options
| Option | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| --keep-sessions | Never kill tmux sessions |
| --keep-sessions-on-fail | Keep sessions only for failed repos (default: true) |
| --attach-on-fail | Print tmux attach hint for failures |
| --capture-lines=N | Lines to capture from pane (default: 400) |

## Pane Output Capture
capture_pane_tail() uses: tmux capture-pane -t session:0.1 -p -S -N
Always captured BEFORE killing session.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-06T21:46:42.819119093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T05:31:22.175022128Z","closed_at":"2026-01-07T05:31:22.175022128Z","close_reason":"All implementation tasks completed - features working and tested","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1vfe","depends_on_id":"bd-bx6s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T21:59:20.150985686Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2","title":"Phase 2: Core Script Skeleton","description":"**EPIC: Main Script Architecture & Entry Point**

## Goal
Create the ru script skeleton with proper shell conventions, argument parsing, subcommand dispatch, and the foundational patterns that all features will build upon.

## Rationale
The architecture decisions made here ripple through the entire codebase. Getting the error handling pattern right (NOT using set -e globally), establishing stream separation (stderr for humans, stdout for data), and setting up proper exit traps prevents countless bugs later.

## Critical Patterns
- `set -uo pipefail` but NOT `set -e` - we handle errors explicitly
- EXIT trap for cleanup and guaranteed summary output
- Subcommand dispatch pattern allows future extensibility
- Global state variables clearly defined at top

## Why Not set -e?
The `set -e` trap is subtle: `output=$(failing_cmd); exit_code=$?` exits before capturing the exit code. We need repos to continue processing even when one fails, so explicit error handling is mandatory.

## Success Criteria
- Script runs without syntax errors
- `ru --help` and `ru --version` work
- Subcommand routing works (even if subcommands are stubs)
- EXIT trap fires and cleans up temp files","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T21:10:05.881422408Z","updated_at":"2026-01-03T21:16:38.688364552Z","closed_at":"2026-01-03T21:16:38.688364552Z","close_reason":"Phase 2 epic - starting core script skeleton implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["architecture","core"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2","depends_on_id":"bd-1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T21:12:12.031401748Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-201","title":"Create ru script with shebang and header","description":"**Create ru script with proper shell header**

## What
Create the ru script file with shebang, metadata header, and `set -uo pipefail`.

## Why
The header establishes the contract: what this script is, what it does, how to use it. The shebang ensures correct interpreter. `set -uo pipefail` catches common errors without the pitfalls of `set -e`.

## Critical: NOT set -e
We explicitly avoid `set -e` because:
- `output=$(failing_cmd); exit_code=$?` exits before capturing exit code
- We need processing to continue after individual repo failures
- Explicit error handling is more predictable

## Header Content
- Script name and purpose
- Feature list
- Usage synopsis
- Command overview
- Global options

## Acceptance Criteria
- ru file exists with executable permission
- Shebang is `#!/usr/bin/env bash`
- Header documents all commands and options
- `set -uo pipefail` is present, `set -e` is NOT","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T21:10:05.881422408Z","updated_at":"2026-01-03T21:18:26.311917815Z","closed_at":"2026-01-03T21:18:26.311917815Z","close_reason":"Implemented in initial ru script skeleton","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["core"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-201","depends_on_id":"bd-101","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T21:14:08.945661914Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-201","depends_on_id":"bd-2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T21:14:08.916838124Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-202","title":"Add VERSION and XDG path constants","description":"**Define VERSION and XDG-compliant path constants**

## What
Add constants for VERSION (read from file), repository info, and XDG paths.

## Why
Centralized constants prevent magic strings scattered through code. XDG paths ensure we respect system conventions.

## Constants Needed
```bash

REPO_OWNER="Dicklesworthstone"
REPO_NAME="repo_updater"

XDG_CONFIG_HOME="${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}"
XDG_DATA_HOME="${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}"
XDG_CACHE_HOME="${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}"
XDG_STATE_HOME="${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}"

RU_CONFIG_DIR="${RU_CONFIG_DIR:-$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ru}"
RU_DATA_DIR="${RU_DATA_DIR:-$XDG_DATA_HOME/ru}"
RU_CACHE_DIR="${RU_CACHE_DIR:-$XDG_CACHE_HOME/ru}"
RU_STATE_DIR="${RU_STATE_DIR:-$XDG_STATE_HOME/ru}"
RU_LOG_DIR="${RU_LOG_DIR:-$RU_STATE_DIR/logs}"
```

 {"id":"bd-cutq","title":"Implement per-repo review policy configuration","description":"# Task: Implement Per-Repo Review Policy Configuration

## Purpose
Allow users to customize review behavior per repository through configuration files. Different repos have different test commands, lint requirements, and review policies.

## Background
Not all repos are the same:
- Some have Makefile, others use npm
- Some require strict lint, others are more lenient
- Some repos should never auto-push
- Some repos need specific branch protection rules

## Configuration Structure

### Directory Layout
```
~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/
├── _default.conf          # Default policy (applies to all)
├── owner_repo1.conf       # Policy for owner/repo1
├── owner_repo2.conf       # Policy for owner/repo2
└── team_*.conf            # Glob patterns work too
```

### Policy File Format
```bash
# ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/myorg_backend.conf

# Test configuration
REVIEW_TEST_CMD="make test"
REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT=300          # seconds

# Lint configuration
REVIEW_LINT_CMD="npm run lint"
REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=true        # Block push if lint fails

# Secret scanning
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
REVIEW_SECRET_PATTERNS="AWS_|PRIVATE_KEY|password"

# Push policy
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true           # false = never push for this repo
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false    # true = always ask before push

# Priority overrides
REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=50          # Add to all items from this repo
REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST="urgent:30,security:40"

# Review behavior
REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=10              # Max items to review per session
REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false            # true = only review issues
REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false           # true = comprehensive review
```

### Default Policy
```bash
# ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/_default.conf

REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""               # Auto-detect
REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""
REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=false
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false
REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=0
REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=20
REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false
REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false
```

## Implementation

### load_repo_policy()
```bash
load_repo_policy() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local policy_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d"
    
    # Reset to defaults first
    source_default_policy
    
    # Try exact match
    local exact_file="$policy_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.conf"
    if [[ -f "$exact_file" ]]; then
        source "$exact_file"
        log_verbose "Loaded policy: $exact_file"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Try glob patterns (owner_*.conf, team_*.conf)
    local owner="${repo_id%%/*}"
    for pattern_file in "$policy_dir"/"${owner}"_*.conf "$policy_dir"/*_*.conf; do
        if [[ -f "$pattern_file" ]]; then
            # Check if pattern matches
            local pattern_base
            pattern_base=$(basename "$pattern_file" .conf)
            if [[ "$repo_id" == ${pattern_base//_/\/} || \
                  "$repo_id" == ${pattern_base//_/}* ]]; then
                source "$pattern_file"
                log_verbose "Loaded policy via pattern: $pattern_file"
                return 0
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    log_verbose "Using default policy for $repo_id"
    return 0
}
```

### source_default_policy()
```bash
source_default_policy() {
    local default_file="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d/_default.conf"
    
    # Built-in defaults
    REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""
    REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT=300
    REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""
    REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=false
    REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
    REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true
    REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false
    REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=0
    REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST=""
    REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=20
    REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false
    REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false
    
    # Override with user defaults if present
    [[ -f "$default_file" ]] && source "$default_file"
}
```

### apply_policy_priority_boost()
```bash
apply_policy_priority_boost() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local base_score="$2"
    local labels="$3"
    
    load_repo_policy "$repo_id"
    
    local score=$base_score
    
    # Add base priority boost
    score=$((score + REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY))
    
    # Apply label boosts
    if [[ -n "$REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST" ]]; then
        IFS="," read -ra boosts <<< "$REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST"
        for boost in "${boosts[@]}"; do
            local label="${boost%%:*}"
            local points="${boost##*:}"
            if echo "$labels" | grep -qi "$label"; then
                score=$((score + points))
            fi
        done
    fi
    
    echo "$score"
}
```

### validate_policy_file()
```bash
validate_policy_file() {
    local file="$1"
    
    # Check syntax
    if ! bash -n "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_error "Syntax error in policy file: $file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check for required variables
    source "$file"
    
    # Validate values
    if [[ -n "$REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT" ]] && ! [[ "$REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        log_error "Invalid REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT in $file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}
```

### init_review_policies()
```bash
init_review_policies() {
    local policy_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$policy_dir" ]]; then
        mkdir -p "$policy_dir"
        
        # Create example default policy
        cat > "$policy_dir/_default.conf.example" << "EOF"
# Default review policy (rename to _default.conf to activate)
# These settings apply to all repos unless overridden

REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""               # Auto-detect (Makefile, package.json, etc.)
REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""               # Optional lint command
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true          # Scan for secrets before push
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true           # Allow pushing changes
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false    # Require confirmation before push
EOF
        log_info "Created example policy file: $policy_dir/_default.conf.example"
    fi
}
```

## Integration Points
- Called by priority scoring (apply_policy_priority_boost)
- Called by quality gates (load test/lint commands)
- Called by push phase (check REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH)
- Called during cmd_review init

## Testing
- Verify default policy applied
- Verify exact match policy overrides default
- Verify glob pattern matching works
- Verify invalid policy files rejected
- Verify priority boosts applied correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default policy applied to all repos
- [ ] Per-repo policies override defaults
- [ ] Glob patterns work for org-wide settings
- [ ] Policy validation catches errors
- [ ] Example policy created on init

## Parent Epic: bd-1vfe (State Management & Artifacts)

## Purpose
Enable resume capability for interrupted sweeps.

## State File Location
~/.local/state/ru/agent_sweep_state.json

## Schema

```json
{
  "run_id": "20260106-153000-12345",
  "status": "in_progress|completed|interrupted",
  "started_at": "2026-01-06T15:30:00Z",
  "config_hash": "abc123...",
  "with_release": false,
  "repos_total": 5,
  "repos_completed": ["repo1", "repo2"],
  "repos_pending": ["repo3", "repo4", "repo5"],
  "current_repo": "repo3",
  "current_phase": 2
}
```

## Implementation

```bash
save_agent_sweep_state() {
    local status="$1"
    local state_file="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/state.json"
    local tmp_file="${state_file}.tmp.$$"
    
    # Prefer jq/python for safe JSON generation
    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        jq -n --arg run_id "$RUN_ID" --arg status "$status" \
            --argjson completed "$(printf "%s\n" "${COMPLETED_REPOS[@]}" | jq -R . | jq -s .)" \
            "{run_id:\$run_id,status:\$status,repos_completed:\$completed}" > "$tmp_file"
    # ... fallbacks for python3, manual JSON
    fi
    
    mv "$tmp_file" "$state_file"
}

load_agent_sweep_state() {
    local state_file="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/state.json"
    [[ \! -f "$state_file" ]] && return 1
    
    RUN_ID=$(json_get_field "$(cat "$state_file")" "run_id")
    COMPLETED_REPOS=($(json_get_field "$(cat "$state_file")" "repos_completed" | jq -r ".[]"))
    # ...
    return 0
}

filter_completed_repos() {
    local -n repos_ref=$1
    local filtered=()
    for repo in "${repos_ref[@]}"; do
        if \! array_contains COMPLETED_REPOS "$repo"; then
            filtered+=("$repo")
        fi
    done
    repos_ref=("${filtered[@]}")
}

cleanup_agent_sweep_state() {
    rm -f "${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/state.json"
}
```

Always write to temp file, then mv (atomic on POSIX).

```bash
If a file is missing, explicitly note that and continue.

RU_UNDERSTANDING_JSON_END'
```

## Phase 2 Prompt (Commit Plan)

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE2_PROMPT='Now, based on your knowledge of the project, DO NOT run git commands.
Instead, produce a COMMIT PLAN as JSON between these markers:
RU_COMMIT_PLAN_JSON_BEGIN
{ ... }
RU_COMMIT_PLAN_JSON_END

Rules:
- Do not edit any code or files.
- Do not include ephemeral/ignored files (.pyc, node_modules, __pycache__, etc.).
- Group changes into logically connected commits.
- For each commit, include:
  - "files": explicit list of paths to stage
  - "message": full commit message (subject + body)
- Include "push": true/false
- Include "excluded_files": list of files excluded and why
Use ultrathink.'
```

## Phase 3 Prompt (Release Plan)

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE3_PROMPT='If a release is warranted based on the changes, DO NOT execute release commands.
Produce a RELEASE PLAN as JSON between:
RU_RELEASE_PLAN_JSON_BEGIN
{ ... }
RU_RELEASE_PLAN_JSON_END

Include:
- "version": proposed version (or null if no release needed)
- "tag": proposed tag (or null)
- "changelog_entry": text to add to CHANGELOG
- "version_files": files to update with new version
- "checks": actions to verify before release (tests/CI)
Use ultrathink.'
```

 {"id":"bd-ix9c","title":"Implement run_sequential_agent_sweep()","description":"# Sequential Agent Sweep Implementation

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Process repositories one at a time (default mode, safest).

## Implementation

```bash
run_sequential_agent_sweep() {
    local -n repos_ref=$1
    local with_release="$2"
    local success_count=0
    local fail_count=0
    
    for repo_spec in "${repos_ref[@]}"; do
        local repo_name repo_path session_name
        repo_name=$(basename "$repo_spec" | sed "s/@.*//")
        repo_path=$(repo_spec_to_path "$repo_spec")
        session_name="ru_sweep_${repo_name//[^a-zA-Z0-9_]/_}_$$"
        
        log_step "Processing: $repo_name"
        
        # Run preflight check
        if \! repo_preflight_check "$repo_path"; then
            log_warn "  Skipping: $PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"
            write_result "$repo_name" "agent-sweep" "preflight_failed" "0" "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON" "$repo_path"
            continue
        fi
        
        if run_single_agent_workflow "$session_name" "$repo_path" "$with_release"; then
            log_success "  Completed: $repo_name"
            ((success_count++))
            mark_repo_completed "$repo_spec"
        else
            log_error "  Failed: $repo_name"
            ((fail_count++))
        fi
        
        save_agent_sweep_state "in_progress"
    done
    
    SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT=$success_count
    SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT=$fail_count
    
    [[ $fail_count -gt 0 ]] && return 1
    return 0
}
```

 {"id":"bd-jen3","title":"Implement review command exit codes and error classification","description":"# Task: Implement Review Command Exit Codes

## Purpose
Define and implement consistent exit codes for `ru review` following the ru exit code conventions, enabling automation and scripting.

## Background: ru Exit Code Convention
From AGENTS.md, ru uses these exit codes:
- 0 = Success (all repos synced/reviewed)
- 1 = Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2 = Conflicts exist (needs resolution)
- 3 = Dependency/system error (gh missing, auth failed)
- 4 = Invalid arguments
- 5 = Interrupted (use --resume)

## Review-Specific Exit Codes

### Exit 0: Success
- All sessions completed successfully
- All questions answered
- All approved changes applied (if --apply)

### Exit 1: Partial Failure
- Some sessions failed but others succeeded
- Some repos could not be reviewed (network, rate limit)
- Partial progress was made

### Exit 2: Conflicts/Review Needed
- Agent detected merge conflicts in worktree
- Quality gates failed (tests/lint)
- Changes require human intervention before apply

### Exit 3: Dependency/System Error
- Claude Code not installed or not in PATH
- gh CLI not authenticated
- tmux not available (for local driver)
- ntm not available (when --mode=ntm specified)

### Exit 4: Invalid Arguments
- Unknown flags
- Invalid --mode value
- Invalid --priority value
- Conflicting options (--plan with --push)

### Exit 5: Interrupted
- SIGINT/SIGTERM received
- --max-runtime exceeded
- --max-questions exceeded
- Checkpoint saved, use --resume to continue

## Implementation

### Error Classification Function
```bash
classify_review_error() {
    local error_type="$1"
    local context="$2"
    
    case "$error_type" in
        session_failed|rate_limited|network_error)
            echo "partial"  # Exit 1
            ;;
        merge_conflict|quality_gate_failed|tests_failed)
            echo "conflict"  # Exit 2
            ;;
        missing_dependency|auth_failed|no_driver)
            echo "system"  # Exit 3
            ;;
        invalid_flag|bad_mode|conflicting_options)
            echo "invalid"  # Exit 4
            ;;
        interrupted|timeout|max_questions)
            echo "interrupted"  # Exit 5
            ;;
        *)
            echo "unknown"
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Exit Code Aggregation
```bash
aggregate_exit_code() {
    local -a exit_codes=("$@")
    local max_code=0
    
    for code in "${exit_codes[@]}"; do
        [[ $code -gt $max_code ]] && max_code=$code
    done
    
    # Special case: 5 (interrupted) takes precedence over 1 (partial)
    if [[ " ${exit_codes[*]} " =~ " 5 " ]]; then
        echo 5
        return
    fi
    
    echo $max_code
}
```

### Final Exit Handling
```bash
finalize_review_exit() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    
    case "$exit_code" in
        0) log_success "Review completed successfully" ;;
        1) log_warn "Review completed with partial failures" ;;
        2) log_error "Review blocked by conflicts - manual resolution needed" ;;
        3) log_error "Review failed due to system/dependency error" ;;
        4) log_error "Invalid arguments" ;;
        5) log_warn "Review interrupted - use --resume to continue" ;;
    esac
    
    exit "$exit_code"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify each exit code for its conditions
- Verify --resume works after exit 5
- Verify aggregation chooses worst code
- Verify CI can parse exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 exit codes defined and documented
- [ ] Error classification works correctly
- [ ] Exit code aggregation from multiple sessions
- [ ] Clear log message for each exit code
- [ ] --resume only available after exit 5

 {"id":"bd-jhx3","title":"Implement test runner detection","description":"# Task: Test runner detection

## What
Detect the appropriate test command for a repo based on its package manager and config files.

## Detection Rules
| Manager | Check For | Test Command |
|---------|-----------|--------------|
| npm | package.json scripts.test | npm test |
| npm | jest.config.* | npx jest |
| pip | pytest.ini, conftest.py | pytest |
| pip | setup.py | python -m pytest |
| cargo | Cargo.toml | cargo test |
| go | *_test.go files | go test ./... |
| make | Makefile with test target | make test |

## Fallback Priority
1. Check package.json scripts.test (npm/node projects)
2. Check Makefile for test target
3. Check for pytest markers
4. Check for *_test.* files
5. Return empty string if no test runner found

## Interface
`detect_test_command <repo_path>` -> string (test command or empty)

## Output Examples
- `/data/projects/foo` with package.json → `npm test`
- `/data/projects/bar` with Cargo.toml → `cargo test`
- `/data/projects/baz` with no tests → `` (empty)

## Considerations
- Some projects have multiple test commands (unit, integration, e2e)
- Default to the most common/quickest test suite
- Allow override via --test-cmd flag in dep-update","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-14T07:07:05.034698229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T08:10:47.433935573Z","closed_at":"2026-01-14T08:10:47.433935573Z","close_reason":"Implemented detect_test_command function with support for npm/jest/vitest, cargo, go, pytest, ruby/rspec, maven, gradle, make, and composer","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"bd-jhxw","title":"Create Unit Tests for update-formula.sh Script","description":"# Create Unit Tests for update-formula.sh Script

## Background

The `update-formula.sh` script in homebrew-tap is critical infrastructure that:
1. Fetches checksums from GitHub releases
2. Updates version numbers in Ruby formula files
3. Updates SHA256 hashes for multiple architectures
4. Handles different formula types (simple vs architecture-specific)

This script has **NO unit tests** currently. Any bug in this script can corrupt formulas, break installations for all users, and require manual recovery.

## Test Coverage Goals

### 1. Version Extraction Tests

```bash
test_version_extraction_from_github_api() {
    # Mock GitHub API response
    local mock_response='{"tag_name": "v0.1.55"}'
    local version=$(extract_version "$mock_response")
    assert_equals "0.1.55" "$version" "Should strip v prefix"
}

test_version_extraction_handles_semver() {
    assert_equals "1.2.3" $(extract_version '{"tag_name": "v1.2.3"}')
    assert_equals "0.0.1" $(extract_version '{"tag_name": "v0.0.1"}')
    assert_equals "10.20.30" $(extract_version '{"tag_name": "v10.20.30"}')
}

test_version_extraction_handles_no_v_prefix() {
    local mock_response='{"tag_name": "0.1.55"}'
    local version=$(extract_version "$mock_response")
    assert_equals "0.1.55" "$version" "Should handle tags without v prefix"
}
```

### 2. Checksum Fetching Tests

```bash
test_checksum_fetch_parses_sha256sums_file() {
    # Mock checksum file content
    local mock_checksums="abc123def456  cass-darwin-amd64.tar.gz
789xyz000111  cass-darwin-arm64.tar.gz
fedcba654321  cass-linux-amd64.tar.gz"
    
    local darwin_amd64=$(parse_checksum "$mock_checksums" "darwin-amd64")
    assert_equals "abc123def456" "$darwin_amd64"
}

test_checksum_fetch_handles_missing_arch() {
    local mock_checksums="abc123  tool-linux-amd64.tar.gz"
    local result=$(parse_checksum "$mock_checksums" "darwin-arm64")
    assert_equals "" "$result" "Should return empty for missing arch"
}

test_checksum_fetch_network_error_handling() {
    # Simulate network failure
    local result
    result=$(fetch_checksums "https://invalid.example.com/404" 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$result" "ERROR" "Should report error on network failure"
}
```

### 3. Formula Update Tests

```bash
test_formula_update_changes_version() {
    # Create temp formula file
    local temp_formula=$(mktemp)
    cat > "$temp_formula" << 'FORMULA'
class Cass < Formula
  version "0.1.50"
  # ... rest of formula
end
FORMULA

    update_formula_version "$temp_formula" "0.1.55"
    local new_version=$(grep 'version' "$temp_formula" | head -1)
    assert_contains "$new_version" "0.1.55"
    rm "$temp_formula"
}

test_formula_update_preserves_structure() {
    # Ensure update doesnt corrupt formula
    local temp_formula=$(mktemp)
    cp "Formula/cass.rb" "$temp_formula"
    
    update_formula_version "$temp_formula" "99.99.99"
    
    # Validate Ruby syntax
    ruby -c "$temp_formula" 2>&1
    assert_command_succeeds "ruby -c $temp_formula" "Formula should remain valid Ruby"
    rm "$temp_formula"
}

test_formula_update_handles_multi_arch() {
    # Test architecture-specific formula updates
    local temp_formula=$(mktemp)
    cat > "$temp_formula" << 'FORMULA'
class Xf < Formula
  on_macos do
    on_intel do
      sha256 "old_hash_intel"
    end
    on_arm do
      sha256 "old_hash_arm"
    end
  end
end
FORMULA

    update_formula_hash "$temp_formula" "darwin-amd64" "new_hash_intel"
    update_formula_hash "$temp_formula" "darwin-arm64" "new_hash_arm"
    
    assert_contains "$(cat $temp_formula)" "new_hash_intel"
    assert_contains "$(cat $temp_formula)" "new_hash_arm"
    rm "$temp_formula"
}
```

### 4. URL Construction Tests

```bash
test_url_construction_for_tar_gz() {
    local url=$(construct_release_url "cass" "0.1.55" "darwin-amd64" "tar.gz")
    assert_equals \
        "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search/releases/download/v0.1.55/cass-darwin-amd64.tar.gz" \
        "$url"
}

test_url_construction_for_exe() {
    local url=$(construct_release_url "cm" "0.2.3" "windows-x64" "exe")
    assert_equals \
        "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/cass_memory_system/releases/download/v0.2.3/cass-memory-windows-x64.exe" \
        "$url"
}
```

### 5. Edge Case Tests

```bash
test_handles_empty_version() {
    local result
    result=$(update_formula_version "Formula/cass.rb" "" 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$result" "ERROR" "Should reject empty version"
}

test_handles_malformed_checksum() {
    local mock_checksums="not-a-valid-checksum-line"
    local result=$(parse_checksum "$mock_checksums" "darwin-amd64")
    assert_equals "" "$result" "Should handle malformed checksum gracefully"
}

test_handles_formula_not_found() {
    local result
    result=$(update_formula_version "/nonexistent/formula.rb" "1.0.0" 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$result" "ERROR" "Should report error for missing formula"
}

test_idempotent_update() {
    # Running update twice with same version should be safe
    local temp_formula=$(mktemp)
    cp "Formula/cass.rb" "$temp_formula"
    
    update_formula_version "$temp_formula" "0.1.55"
    local hash1=$(sha256sum "$temp_formula")
    
    update_formula_version "$temp_formula" "0.1.55"
    local hash2=$(sha256sum "$temp_formula")
    
    assert_equals "$hash1" "$hash2" "Same update should be idempotent"
    rm "$temp_formula"
}
```

### 6. Logging Tests

```bash
test_logs_include_timestamp() {
    LOG_FORMAT=json ./update-formula.sh cass 0.1.55 --dry-run 2>&1 | head -1 > /tmp/log_line.json
    assert_command_succeeds "jq -e .timestamp /tmp/log_line.json" "Log should include timestamp"
}

test_logs_include_tool_name() {
    LOG_FORMAT=json ./update-formula.sh cass 0.1.55 --dry-run 2>&1 | head -1 > /tmp/log_line.json
    local tool=$(jq -r .tool /tmp/log_line.json)
    assert_equals "cass" "$tool" "Log should include tool name"
}
```

## Test File Location

`/data/projects/homebrew-tap/tests/unit/test_update_formula.sh`

## Dependencies

- Requires test framework (bd-p5x1) to be implemented first
- Uses mock data/fixtures instead of real network calls where possible
- Should run in CI and locally

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All test functions implemented
- [ ] Tests pass on clean homebrew-tap checkout
- [ ] Tests run in < 30 seconds total
- [ ] Coverage report shows > 80% line coverage of update-formula.sh
- [ ] CI workflow runs tests on every PR
- [ ] Tests use JSON logging in CI mode

## Detailed Logging Requirements

Each test MUST log:
1. Test name and description
2. Input values/fixtures used
3. Expected vs actual results
4. Duration in milliseconds
5. Any cleanup actions taken

 {"id":"bd-tcns","title":"Design agent prompts for review workflow","description":"Task: Design Agent Prompts for Review Workflow

Purpose
-------
Craft the prompts that instruct Claude on how to review issues/PRs, what
constraints to follow (no gh mutations), and what artifacts to produce.

Background: Prompt Engineering Matters
--------------------------------------
The quality of automated reviews depends heavily on prompt design:
- Clear policy communication (no PRs accepted)
- Explicit constraints (Plan mode = no mutations)
- Required output format (review-plan.json)
- Independent verification emphasis

Prompt Components
-----------------

1. System Context (AGENTS.md Reading)
   First read ALL of AGENTS.md and README.md carefully.
   Use your code investigation agent mode to fully understand the code,
   technical architecture, and purpose of the project. Use ultrathink.

2. Digest Handling
   If prior digest exists: read it, update based on delta commits
   If no digest: create comprehensive fresh digest
   Always write updated digest to .ru/repo-digest.md

3. Policy Communication
   POLICY: We do not accept PRs or outside contributions. The maintainer
   policy is disclosed to users. Claude reviews and independently decides
   whether and how to address submissions. Bug reports are welcome.

4. Review Instructions
   For each work item:
   - Read via gh issue view or gh pr view
   - Verify claims independently - do not trust user reports blindly
   - Check dates against recent commits (many issues may be stale)
   - If actionable: create local commits with fixes/features
   - If unclear: prepare question for maintainer

5. Plan Mode Constraints (CRITICAL)
   - DO NOT run gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr comment, etc.
   - DO NOT push any changes
   - Only use gh for READ operations (view, list)
   - All mutations will be applied by ru in a separate phase

6. Required Output
   Must produce .ru/review-plan.json with schema:
   {
     "schema_version": 1,
     "run_id": "...",
     "repo": "owner/repo",
     "items": [...],
     "questions": [...],
     "git": {...},
     "gh_actions": [...]
   }

Prompt Template Function
------------------------
generate_review_prompt() takes:
- wt_path: worktree path
- repo_name: owner/repo format
- work_items: JSON array of items to review
- run_id: unique review run ID

Checks for existing digest and adjusts instructions accordingly.

Prompt Variations
-----------------
- Quick Review: Focus on high-priority items, target 10 minutes
- Deep Review: Comprehensive analysis, architectural implications
- Security-Focused: Security items first, verify all claims

Testing
-------
- Verify Claude produces valid review-plan.json
- Verify gh mutations are NOT attempted
- Verify digest is created/updated
- Verify questions use AskUserQuestion tool

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Prompt template produces working reviews
- [ ] Plan mode constraints enforced
- [ ] Review plan artifact created correctly
- [ ] Policy communicated clearly
- [ ] Digest handling works both cases
- [ ] Questions formatted for aggregation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T20:37:17.313860817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:44:47.540328214Z","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:44:47.540328214Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tcns","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T20:44:54.883229859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-tfms","title":"Implement agent-sweep command for automated dirty repo processing","description":"## Summary
The `agent-sweep` command is documented in SKILL.md but not yet implemented in ru. This feature would orchestrate AI coding agents to automatically process repositories with uncommitted changes.

## Requirements from SKILL.md

### Basic Usage
```bash
ru agent-sweep              # Process all repos with uncommitted changes
ru agent-sweep --dry-run    # Preview what would be processed
ru agent-sweep -j4          # Process 4 repos in parallel
ru agent-sweep --repos="myproject*"  # Filter to specific repos
ru agent-sweep --with-release  # Include release step after commit
ru agent-sweep --resume     # Resume interrupted sweep
ru agent-sweep --restart    # Start fresh
```

### Three-Phase Agent Workflow
1. **Phase 1: Planning** - Claude Code analyzes uncommitted changes, determines staging, generates commit message
2. **Phase 2: Commit** - Validates plan, stages files, creates commit, runs quality gates, optionally pushes
3. **Phase 3: Release** (optional) - Analyzes history, determines version bump, creates tag/release

+## 🤖 AI Sync
+
+The `ai-sync` subcommand automatically commits uncommitted changes across repositories using AI agents. It scans for dirty repos, launches an AI agent per repo (via ntm), and the agent reads project context, reviews changes, creates logical commits, and pushes.
+
+### Prerequisites
+
+- **ntm** (Named Tmux Manager) - for session orchestration
+- **claude-code**, **codex**, or **gemini** CLI - for AI agent execution
+
+### Usage
+
+```bash
+ru ai-sync                        # Process all dirty repos
+ru ai-sync --dry-run              # Show what would be processed
+ru ai-sync --include=my-project   # Only process matching repos
+ru ai-sync --exclude=test-*       # Skip matching repos
+ru ai-sync --timeout=1200         # Allow 20 minutes per repo
+ru ai-sync --no-push              # Commit but don't push
+ru ai-sync --agent=codex          # Use a different AI agent
+ru ai-sync --no-untracked         # Ignore untracked files
+```
+
+### How It Works
+
+1. Scans all configured repos for uncommitted changes
+2. For each dirty repo, launches an AI agent session via ntm
+3. The agent reads `AGENTS.md` and `README.md` to understand the project
+4. Reviews all changes and groups them into logical commits
+5. Creates detailed commit messages explaining the changes
+6. Pushes to the remote (unless `--no-push` is specified)
+
+---
+
 ## 📦 Dependency Updates
 
 The `dep-update` subcommand uses AI agents to automatically update dependencies across your repositories. It analyzes changelogs for breaking changes, creates migration plans, updates dependencies one at a time, runs tests, and commits successful updates.
